stories.title: 'Love Island USA' star Olandria Carthen's best red carpet looks
stories.summary: Love Island USA star Olandria Carthen and her boyfriend Nic Vansteenberghe, also known as Nicolandria, have broken up after nearly a year together. The couple, who met on the reality show's season 7, cited their busy schedules as the reason for their split. They remain close friends and will continue to support each other. This news comes as Love Island USA season 8 has recently concluded.

stories.title: How Pete Golding described relationship with Lane Kiffin after leaving Ole Miss for LSU - The Clarion-Ledger
stories.summary: Ole Miss coach Pete Golding said he and former coach Lane Kiffin are on good terms despite Kiffin's dramatic exit for LSU last season. Golding, who took over as head coach after Kiffin's departure, signed a five-year deal with the Rebels and led them to two College Football Playoff victories. The two will face off in a highly anticipated Week 3 matchup on September 19 at Vaught-Hemingway Stadium. Golding also addressed the college football transfer portal system, suggesting it can put players in bad positions.

stories.title: Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC launches today with Blinx, Conker and more
stories.summary: Microsoft launches Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC today, allowing original Xbox games to be played on PC and handheld devices. Four classic games, including Blinx: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fuzion Frenzy, are available to play at no extra charge for those with digital Xbox versions. The PC versions feature up to 4x resolution upscaling and other graphics enhancements. This marks the start of a wider preservation effort, with more classic Xbox games headed to PC.

stories.title: Hiker injured in ‘surprise encounter’ with bear on brushy South Anchorage trail
stories.summary: A woman was hospitalized after being attacked by a brown bear while hiking in the Bear Valley area of South Anchorage on July 18. The bear, which emerged from a dense stand of alder trees, made brief contact with one of the hikers before moving on. The hiker sustained non-life-threatening injuries and was transported to a local hospital. Authorities believe the encounter was likely a surprise due to high winds and inclement weather conditions.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Arrowhead Drug Shows Strong Efficacy In Cutting Blood Fat
stories.summary: Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals reports successful results from its late-stage trials for the treatment of severe hypertriglyceridemia, with patients receiving the drug experiencing a median triglyceride decrease of 79-81% after 12 months, compared to 27% in the placebo group. The trials, SHASTA-3 and SHASTA-4, achieved their primary milestone and all secondary objectives. This breakthrough could significantly reduce the risk of acute pancreatitis in patients with high blood fat levels. The company's stock is trading higher following the announcement.

stories.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing them as a "signifier of modernity" that can disrupt the on-screen reality. This policy was evident in his recent film "The Odyssey," where actors wore traditional Ancient Greek attire. The ban applies to all his productions, including future projects. The decision aims to maintain the authenticity of his historical dramas.

stories.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
stories.summary: Actress Sophie Thatcher is stepping out of the darkness after being typecast as a troubled character in her recent roles, including Natalie in the hit series "Yellowjackets." The 23-year-old actress has appeared in several films and TV shows, showcasing her versatility in complex and often dark characters. Thatcher is now looking to move beyond these portrayals and explore new roles. Her next project remains undisclosed, but fans are eager to see her take on fresh challenges.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: The UK's Conservative Party has asked HMRC to investigate a £5m gift to Nigel Farage, questioning whether it constitutes taxable income. The gift was given by crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne, who claims it was a personal and unconditional donation. Farage has previously stated the money would cover personal security costs and compensate him for campaigning for Brexit. HMRC will now review the matter to determine if Farage owes tax on the gift.

stories.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar has resigned to join Andy Burnham's government, becoming the first senior Labour figure to be appointed to a ministerial post in the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. He will also be made a peer. The move has been met with criticism from Scottish rivals, who accuse Sarwar of hypocrisy and lying to voters. The SNP is calling for Sarwar to donate his new salary to charity.

stories.title: Isco se queda fuera de la lista de Pellegrini para el Colombino
stories.summary: Betis captain Isco has been left out of Manuel Pellegrini's squad for the team's second pre-season friendly against Recreativo in Huelva's LVII Tropeo Colombino. The match takes place today at 8:30 PM local time. Isco missed the team's training session in Germany and only rejoined his teammates two days ago. He will not participate in today's game, along with Brazilian player Antony and Abde, who are still getting back into shape.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility, the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years, will be open to a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials will be available for a brief media availability. The event is in person only and open to US citizens or lawful permanent residents.

stories.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham is facing controversy over his handling of policy decisions, including a potential income tax threshold increase, just three days into his tenure. He has been accused of performing U-turns on key issues, sparking speculation and criticism. The prime minister's off-the-cuff remarks have raised questions about his approach to governance. His office is now working to clarify his stance on these policies.

stories.title: FCC Chair Says ABC’s Decision to Not Broadcast Trump Speech Live Will Be Part of Agency’s Review of Station Licenses
stories.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the agency will review the licenses of eight ABC-owned stations after ABC chose not to broadcast President Donald Trump's July 16 speech on US election security live. The decision comes after Trump threatened to revoke the licenses of ABC and NBC for not carrying his remarks live. The review is expected to consider ABC's decision as part of the agency's routine license renewal process. The outcome of the review remains uncertain.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho, helped Celtic stay one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes will serve three games immediately and one suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open their Scottish Premiership campaign.

stories.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision in a May match "disgusting". The incident occurred at Motherwell on May 13, when Celtic was awarded a stoppage-time spot-kick that sealed a 3-2 victory. McInnes will serve the ban, which includes one suspended match, and will be in the stands for Rangers' next Scottish Premiership match. He will contest the decision, arguing his comments were directed at the specific decision rather than a broader criticism of officials.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. He was 69 years old. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. The probe into Siad's alleged involvement in organised human trafficking and conspiracy is continuing, despite his death.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation centers on Casemiro's discovery rights, which the LA Galaxy held before Inter Miami acquired them. The league is reviewing the situation and will release more information once the investigation is complete. The outcome may impact the terms of Casemiro's contract and Inter Miami's roster.

stories.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
stories.summary: A robotic spacecraft, the Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites, has launched from Cape Canaveral on July 21, supported by NASA, and is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit. The mission, funded by DARPA, will use twin robotic arms to inspect and upgrade satellites by installing propulsion modules, extending their operational life. This is the first multi-mission robotic in-space servicer in the US. The spacecraft is expected to demonstrate advanced in-space servicing capabilities.

stories.title: Aston Martin secures £550m loan deal
stories.summary: Aston Martin has secured a £550m loan deal to bolster its finances. The luxury car maker has been struggling with net losses of £493.2m last year, a 50% increase from the previous year. The loan is expected to fund future product plans and bolster its balance sheet. The company will publish its half-year results on July 29, providing further insight into its financial situation.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reversed his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of authority to do so. Mamdani had previously called for US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Netanyahu over alleged war crimes. The US is not a member of the ICC, making it unlikely Netanyahu would be arrested in the country. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump stated Netanyahu would not be arrested while in the US.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
stories.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to reduce obesity in Britain. The committee's findings are based on evidence that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed. The government is expected to consider the report's recommendations in its efforts to address the obesity crisis.

stories.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns, and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and their lawyers requested extradition to Russia. The case marks a significant blow to a Russian political influence operation in Angola.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the stabbing death of a 65-year-old British-South African woman, Karen Carter, in Trémolat, France, last year. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car in April 2023. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She is now formally charged in connection with Carter's death.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 of 33 buildings tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to test the remaining 140 cooling tower systems. Further testing is expected to reveal whether the outbreak is contained.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
